what_it_does: This file is a design breakdown note that captures specified requirements, implied system behavior, and proposed additions for a batched annotation submission feature with visibility, replies, and structured choices.
observations: Uses frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, created/updated timestamps, and history.
Body is organized into SPECIFIED, IMPLIED, and ADDITIONS sections.
SPECIFIED: do not auto-send; compose several items and submit all with one action; need on-screen visibility; need to see replies and make choices.

IMPLIED: client-side draft queue, deliberate submit-all, live status view, structured questions/decisions surfaced for a user to pick, optimistic UI, offline tolerance, reply notifications, editable/removable drafts before submit.

ADDITIONS: edit/delete/reorder drafts; group or label batches; choose target channel/members per batch or per comment; sent log; undo submission; unread-replies badge; submit from anywhere via an overlay.
Links to parent item board://item-11da929d via part_of.
standouts: One-action batch submit designed to feel the same whether submitting 1 or 12 items.
Client-side draft queue held locally until deliberate submit-all.
Tie to a decision-surface where members can pose structured questions/decisions for the user to pick.
Submit-from-anywhere overlay rather than a dedicated screen.
conventions: Follows the ledger item format with YAML frontmatter and markdown body.
Uses explicit SPECIFIED/IMPLIED/ADDITIONS structure to separate requirements from inferred behavior and extra ideas.
References a parent item through a part_of link.
Uses neutral, design-substrate language rather than implementation code.
concerns: ADDITIONS section bundles many unrelated UX capabilities without priority or acceptance criteria.
References to 'members' and 'Tim' are undefined within this file.
Optimistic UI and offline tolerance are stated but not quantified or scoped.
No mention of persistence, session lifecycle, or conflict handling for drafts.
notes: A design breakdown for a batched annotation workflow that emphasizes deliberate send, local drafts, live status, and structured decision replies.
questions: Who or what is 'Tim' in the context of making choices?
What is the exact definition of the 'decision-surface'?
Does the draft queue persist across app sessions or only the current session?
How is offline tolerance implemented and what happens on sync failure?
What is the 'overlay' and where can it be invoked?
How are reply notifications delivered and silenced?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the title and content both describe a breakdown of batched submission, visibility, replies, and choices.
intention_signals: It appears to be designing a low-friction, high-control annotation workflow where users can privately compose multiple comments, submit them as one deliberate batch, then monitor replies and make structured decisions without leaving their current context.
novelty: The deliberate one-action batch submit regardless of item count, combined with an overlay submit-from-anywhere pattern and integration with a decision-surface for structured choices.
contribution: The file contributes a batched draft-queue model with editable, reorderable, deletable drafts; batch grouping and labeling; per-batch or per-comment target channel/member selection; a live status and unread-replies surface; sent log and undo; and an overlay submission pattern.
summary_for_embedding: A ledger design breakdown item defining a batched annotation submission feature with a client-side draft queue, deliberate submit-all action, live status, reply notifications, structured choices, and overlay submission.
intention_for_embedding: This item was created to capture requirements and design implications for a batched, visibility-rich annotation and decision workflow.
